analysis_methodology.upstream_analysis: Identified all programs that directly CALL CUSTOMER-PROC (programs that depend on this artefact)
analysis_methodology.downstream_analysis: Traced all programs that CUSTOMER-PROC calls, including transitive dependencies
analysis_methodology.shared_data_analysis: Analyzed copybook dependencies and identified programs sharing CUSTOMER-RECORD copybook
analysis_methodology.impact_scoring: Applied scoring matrix based on: direct callers (30%), transitive depth (20%), copybook breadth (25%), complexity (15%), risk flags (10%)
analysis_methodology.risk_assessment: Evaluated program complexity, control flow patterns (GOTO, ALTER), and call depth in the dependency chain

call_chain_visualization.description: Complete call hierarchy showing CUSTOMER-PROC in context

affected_programs.reason: Directly calls CUSTOMER-PROC; top-level batch processing entry point; any changes to CUSTOMER-PROC interface or behavior will affect batch workflow

metadata.author: D.WILSON
metadata.remarks: Master batch controller. Reads customer extract file and invokes CUSTOMER-PROC for each record. Produces batch run statistics report.

impact_score.adjusted_level: HIGH (elevated due to entry point role)

key_concerns: Entry point for entire batch processing workflow
No upstream callers - changes here don't cascade further up
Reads BATCH-INPUT file and writes BATCH-REPORT - verify report output
Shares CUSTOMER-RECORD structure with CUSTOMER-PROC - data contract changes will break this program
Must perform full end-to-end batch processing regression test
Verify error handling and statistics reporting after CUSTOMER-PROC changes
specific_regression_tests: Full batch run with sample customer extract file
Verify BATCH-REPORT output format and statistics
Test error handling when CUSTOMER-PROC returns error conditions
Validate CUSTOMER-REC data passed to CUSTOMER-PROC

affected_programs.reason: Directly called by CUSTOMER-PROC; receives CUSTOMER-REC as parameter; MEDIUM complexity with multiple copybook dependencies

copybook_dependencies.shared_data_risk: HIGH - Uses CUSTOMER-RECORD and expects specific data from CUSTOMER-PROC

call_details.data_dependency: Receives customer data structure from CUSTOMER-PROC

key_concerns: Receives CUSTOMER-REC as parameter - interface contract must remain stable
MEDIUM complexity - more extensive logic to validate
Uses 3 different copybooks - complex data dependencies
Calls PAYMENT-HANDLER - changes cascade further downstream
Performs INDEXED file operations on ACCOUNT-FILE - verify data integrity
Multiple paragraph-level operations: validate, update, process payment
Critical business logic for account management and payment processing
specific_regression_tests: Test all account operations: activate, deactivate, close
Verify customer validation logic with modified CUSTOMER-PROC data
Test payment processing flow when called from modified CUSTOMER-PROC
Verify error handling for missing accounts
Confirm return codes and status passed back to CUSTOMER-PROC
Integration test: CUSTOMER-PROC → ACCOUNT-MGR → PAYMENT-HANDLER flow

affected_programs.reason: Called by ACCOUNT-MGR (which is called by CUSTOMER-PROC); indirect impact through data and control flow; contains GOTO statements (legacy risk)

metadata.author: S.PATEL
metadata.remarks: Processes payment transactions. Routes to appropriate handler based on payment type. Legacy GOTO retained from original implementation - flagged for refactoring.

copybook_dependencies.shared_data_risk: LOW - No direct data structure sharing with CUSTOMER-PROC

call_details.data_dependency: Indirectly depends on customer data processed by CUSTOMER-PROC

risk_analysis.control_flow_risk: MEDIUM - GOTO statements make control flow harder to predict
risk_analysis.refactoring_recommendation: Consider eliminating GOTO during any modification cycle

key_concerns: Contains GOTO statements - higher risk of unexpected behavior
Leaf program (no outbound calls) - easier to isolate and test
Payment transaction logging - critical for audit trail
PAYMENT-RECORD copybook used - verify payment data structure
Indirect impact means changes may be subtle - thorough testing required
Multiple payment types (regular, refund, reversal) - test all paths
specific_regression_tests: Test all payment processing paths: regular, refund, reversal
Test GOTO control flow paths explicitly
Validate return codes passed back to ACCOUNT-MGR
Integration test: ensure payment processing still works with modified CUSTOMER-PROC data flow
Test error conditions and invalid payment scenarios

shared_copybook_impact.risk_rationale: CUSTOMER-RECORD is shared by 3 programs including CUSTOMER-PROC. Any modifications to CUSTOMER-PROC that alter how it uses or populates CUSTOMER-RECORD fields will impact other users.

other_copybooks_in_chain.relationship: Used by downstream programs in call chain

target_program_files.risk: MEDIUM - File read logic changes may affect data availability
LOW - Output file, less likely to affect downstream programs

file_io_impact_analysis.file_dependency_risk: MEDIUM - Changes to CUSTOMER-PROC file processing may affect upstream batch processing and downstream account/payment operations

recommended_change_sequence.description: Review proposed CUSTOMER-PROC modifications with team
tasks: Document all code changes to CUSTOMER-PROC
Review interface contract (parameters passed to/from ACCOUNT-MGR)
Verify CUSTOMER-RECORD copybook usage remains consistent
Ensure CUSTOMER-RECORD copybook fields remain consistent
Maintain interface contract with ACCOUNT-MGR (CUSTOMER-REC parameter)
Test error handling and EOF conditions
Validate return codes and status flags
Confirm CUSTOMER-REC structure is correctly populated
Verify return codes and error handling
Test payment processing with modified CUSTOMER-PROC
Verify all payment types (regular, refund, reversal)
Test GOTO control flow paths in PAYMENT-HANDLER

recommended_change_sequence.description: Test the batch processing entry point
tasks: Run full BATCH-RUNNER execution with modified CUSTOMER-PROC
Compile all affected programs if needed
Prepare JCL for batch job execution
Monitor first full production batch cycle

risk_assessment.risk_category: Program modification with upstream and downstream dependencies

risk_factors.description: BATCH-RUNNER is the entry point for batch processing workflow; any interface changes to CUSTOMER-PROC will break batch processing
CUSTOMER-PROC calls ACCOUNT-MGR which calls PAYMENT-HANDLER; changes cascade through 2 downstream programs
CUSTOMER-RECORD copybook shared by 3 programs; data contract must remain stable
Indirect impact on payment processing through PAYMENT-HANDLER
PAYMENT-HANDLER contains GOTO statements making behavior harder to predict
CUSTOMER-PROC itself is LOW complexity, reducing modification risk

actions: Maintain strict backward compatibility with CUSTOMER-REC parameter structure
Do not modify CUSTOMER-RECORD copybook field definitions
Preserve return codes and error handling patterns
Document any behavioral changes clearly
Execute complete regression test suite (all 3 affected programs)
Perform full batch processing integration test
Test all payment processing scenarios
Validate all file I/O operations
Include negative test cases and error scenarios
Deploy to test environment first
Execute pilot batch run with sample data
Monitor for 48 hours in test environment
Use canary deployment if possible
Have rollback plan ready
Add detailed logging to CUSTOMER-PROC modifications
Monitor BATCH-RUNNER statistics closely
Track PAYMENT-LOG for anomalies
Set up alerts for batch job failures
Review audit trail for first week post-deployment
Peer review all code changes
Run static code analysis
Validate against COBOL coding standards
Document all assumptions and dependencies
Consider refactoring GOTO statements in PAYMENT-HANDLER if touched

test_cases: Test EOF and error flag handling
Test with various customer statuses (ACTIVE, INACTIVE, CLOSED)
Test EOF handling in batch context
Test error handling and batch recovery
Test with large batch input files
Test all customer scenarios (active, inactive, closed)
Test all payment types (regular, refund, reversal)
Verify all file operations (6 files total)

test_data_requirements: Customer master file (CUSTMAST) with various customer statuses
Batch input file (BATCHIN) with representative customer data
Account master file (ACCTMAST) with test accounts
Edge cases: invalid customers, closed accounts, missing data
Large volume data for performance testing
environment_requirements: Test mainframe or emulator with COBOL runtime
All 6 files (CUSTMAST, AUDITMAST, BATCHIN, BATCHRPT, ACCTMAST, PAYLOG)
Backup and restore capabilities for test data

assumptions: 2 testers for integration and regression testing
Test environment is available and configured

immediate_actions: Freeze CUSTOMER-RECORD copybook - no modifications allowed during this change cycle
Notify owners of BATCH-RUNNER, ACCOUNT-MGR, and PAYMENT-HANDLER programs
Schedule code review session with technical lead
Prepare test data sets for all scenarios
best_practices: Maintain strict interface compatibility with ACCOUNT-MGR
Add comprehensive inline comments for all changes
Use version control for all source code
Document all assumptions and design decisions
Perform peer review before testing phase
future_considerations: Consider refactoring PAYMENT-HANDLER to eliminate GOTO statements
Evaluate modernization of CUSTOMER-PROC to Java/Spring Boot as part of broader migration
Review performance of batch processing workflow
Consider adding more comprehensive error handling across all programs
Evaluate replacing SEQUENTIAL files with database tables
